definition: The structural floor for core PCE inflation has shifted upward by an estimated 30-80bp to ~2.3-2.8%, driven by housing supply deficit (10-20bp), deglobalization/tariffs (10-20bp), and healthcare/insurance dynamics (10-15bp), though the directional shift is high-confidence while the precise magnitude is genuinely uncertain.
evidence: Housing supply deficit contributing ~10-20bp structural inflation
Direction is high-confidence (~8/10) but magnitude is uncertain (~5/10)
Decomposition methodologies (Beveridge-Nelson, UC-SV, analogue-weighted) produce wide confidence intervals of +/-20-30bp
open_questions: What is the precise magnitude of the structural shift?
How much of above-target inflation is structural vs. cyclical? (Highest-priority unresolved disagreement: estimates range 30-70% structural)
Which decomposition methodology is most reliable?
